label: Delivery model
desc: Who actually produces the outcome. Offers often blend models — check each one in play.
optDesc: You produce the outcome; little effort required from the buyer.
You and the buyer produce the outcome together.
You give the buyer everything they need to produce it themselves.

label: Source of value
desc: What the buyer is really paying for. Most offers draw on two or all three.
optDesc: Your judgment, taste, and expertise.
A process, template, or method that delivers reliably.
A tangible thing you've produced or own (content, IP, product, audience).

label: Interaction timing
desc: When value happens. Checking both is the hybrid.
optDesc: Live, real-time (calls, sessions, events).
On each party's own schedule (messages, recordings).

label: Participation format
desc: The shape of the audience. An offer can hold more than one.
optDesc: One person at a time.
A small, defined group at the same time.
An ongoing ecosystem where many people participate and engage each other.
One effort reaches an unlimited audience (async, at scale).

label: Delivery medium
desc: How value reaches the buyer. Most offers deliver through a mix.
optDesc: Your live time and attention (calls, coaching).
Reusable materials (recorded content, templates, guides, checklists).
Software, a platform, or an interactive product.

label: Pricing model
desc: How the buyer pays. Models stack — a retainer with a setup fee is Fixed + Recurring.
optDesc: One set price for a defined offer.
Price changes with scope, usage, or results.
The buyer pays for time (hours, days, blocks).
The buyer pays repeatedly for continuous value.

label: Price framing
desc: How the price is positioned. Checking both is the hybrid frame.
optDesc: A cost to be minimized.
An investment expected to create measurable upside.

label: Touch intensity
desc: How much direct involvement the engagement carries.
optDesc: Minimal direct involvement.
Some direct support, but limited and structured.
Frequent, direct, personal involvement throughout.

label: Scope breadth
desc: How much ground the offer covers.
optDesc: One specific result or deliverable.
One whole area of the buyer's work or life.
The complete chain connected to a business result.

label: Scope depth
desc: How far below the surface the offer goes.
optDesc: The visible surface problem.
The need and its immediate supporting layers.
Root causes, structural layers, and underlying systems.

label: System impact
desc: How much of the buyer's operation the offer changes.
optDesc: A standalone result; the deliverable stands on its own.
Reshapes a meaningful chunk of how they work.
Changes core business mechanics.

label: Buyer participation intensity
desc: How much work the buyer puts in.
optDesc: Receives the value with little effort of their own.
Contributes meaningful input at key moments.
Actively works throughout the process.

label: Effort duration
optDesc: A single delivery or short burst, then it ends.
A defined period with a set start and end.
Continues indefinitely, renewing until canceled.

label: Commitment reversibility
desc: How easily the buyer can back out.
optDesc: Hard to undo after buying.
Some lock-in; partial refund or wind-down.
Easy to stop, cancel, switch, or back out.

label: Authority dynamic
optDesc: You're the executor; the buyer decides what they want.
You guide; the buyer decides alongside you.
The buyer trusts your judgment; you own the calls.
